claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 4 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 4 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child in care for 4 weeks (within 8-week limit)
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 4 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child in care for 4 weeks (within 8-week limit); Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child in care for 4 weeks (within 8-week limit)
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. Let me first review the official eligibility criteria, and then I'll ask you some questions about your circumstances.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for waiting. I've reviewed the eligibility rules for Child Benefit. To help assess your eligibility, I need to ask you some questions.

First, do you have any children you're responsible for, and if so, how many?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I have one child. Their name is Alex and they're 4 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. 

Do you live with Alex in the UK?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I do. We live together here in Manchester.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's helpful, thank you.

Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing that information. Based on what you've told me, I can now assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

Let me summarize your situation:
- You have one child named Alex who is 4 years old
- You live with Alex in the UK (Manchester)
- Nobody else is currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex

According to the eligibility rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit if you're responsible for a child under 16 and you live in the UK. You're usually considered responsible for a child if you live with them.

Since Alex is 4 years old (under 16), you live together in the UK, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex, you meet the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Good news! You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.**

You meet all the requirements:
- Alex is 4 years old (under 16)
- You live with Alex in the UK
- Nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex

You can proceed with your Child Benefit application. Please note that if you or your partner have an individual income over £60,000, you may need to pay the High Income Child Benefit tax charge, but this doesn't affect your eligibility to claim.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

reasonings: Alex is 4 years old, which is under 16. The claimant lives with Alex in the UK (Manchester), which means they are responsible for the child under the eligibility rules. No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex, so there is no conflict of claims. All eligibility criteria are met: the child is under 16, the claimant is responsible for them by living with them, they reside in the UK, and only one person is claiming for this child.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant has one child, Alex (aged 4), who lives with them in Manchester, UK. No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. The claimant meets all the standard eligibility criteria: they are responsible for a child under 16, they live with the child in the UK, and there are no conflicting claims. Therefore, the claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.

All eligibility criteria are met: the child is under 16, the claimant is responsible for them by living with them, they reside in the UK, and only one person is claiming for this child.

The claimant meets all the standard eligibility criteria: they are responsible for a child under 16, they live with the child in the UK, and there are no conflicting claims. Therefore, the claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.
